title: Pecluma rhachipterygia
taxonomy: Pecluma rhachipterygia is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is classified as a genus of epiphytic ferns, native to tropical regions. The classification of Pecluma rhachipterygia is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, and Family: Polypodiaceae. Within the family Polypodiaceae, Pecluma rhachipterygia is a member of the subfamily Platycarioideae. Further research may be needed to confirm its specific classification within the subfamily.

intro: Pecluma rhachipterygia, also known as the "Mexican fern", is a species of epiphytic fern native to Mexico and Central America. This plant is prized for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve overall immune function. The bioactive constituents of Pecluma rhachipterygia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pecluma rhachipterygia is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is commonly used to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
